techniques.evidence_quote: the initial compromise of the client was a Gitlab vulnerability (CVE-2021-22205)
gained access to sensitive data, which included the access key for an Admin level identity in the victims AWS environment
gained access to sensitive data, which included the access key for an Admin level identity
initial access into the AWS environment was a ListBuckets
logged into AWS console with the newly created backup identity and explored the services
leveraged the stolen access key to CreateUser named backup and CreateAccessKey via S3 Browser
PutUserPolicy to create a policy named backupuser which allowed full privileges to all resources for the identity backup
attacker began to use the AWS web console to create EC2 instances for the purpose of crypto mining

attack_chain.description: Attacker exploited Gitlab vulnerability CVE-2021-22205 to gain access to sensitive data on the victim's Gitlab instance.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the initial compromise of the client was a Gitlab vulnerability (CVE-2021-22205)

evidence_chunks.quote: the initial compromise of the client was a Gitlab vulnerability (CVE-2021-22205)
the initial compromise of the client was a Gitlab vulnerability (CVE-2021-22205)

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained AWS access key for an Admin-level identity from sensitive data exposed via the Gitlab compromise.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: gained access to sensitive data, which included the access key for an Admin level identity in the victims AWS environment

evidence_chunks.quote: gained access to sensitive data, which included the access key for an Admin level identity in the victims AWS environment
gained access to sensitive data, which included the access key for an Admin level identity in the victims AWS environment

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the stolen admin access key to access AWS, issuing ListBuckets from Indonesian IP 182.1.229.252 with S3 Browser 9.5.5 user-agent.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: initial access into the AWS environment was a ListBuckets that came through this access key from the Indonesian IP address 182.1.229.252

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated S3 buckets via ListBuckets using S3 Browser.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: initial access into the AWS environment was a ListBuckets

evidence_chunks.quote: initial access into the AWS environment was a ListBuckets
initial access into the AWS environment was a ListBuckets

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted PutUserPolicy to attach a policy named 'dq' granting s3:GetObject to an existing backup-related identity, but it failed due to the unmodified template resource ARN.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attempted to PutUserPolicy to add a policy named dq to an existing identity associated with backups. The PutUserPolicy attempt failed

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the stolen key to CreateUser named 'backup' to maintain persistent access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: leveraged the stolen access key to CreateUser named backup

attack_chain.description: Attacker generated a new access key (CreateAccessKey) for the newly created 'backup' IAM user.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: CreateUser named backup and CreateAccessKey via S3 Browser

attack_chain.description: Attacker attached inline policy 'backupuser' granting Action:* Resource:* (full admin) to the new 'backup' IAM user.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: PutUserPolicy to create a policy named backupuser which allowed full privileges to all resources for the identity backup

attack_chain.description: Attacker logged into the AWS console as the new 'backup' identity and enumerated services including health, support, ec2, elb, s3, cloudfront, trustedadvisor, monitoring, and compute-optimizer.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: logged into AWS console with the newly created backup identity and explored the services

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: About 31 minutes after initial access, the attacker used the EC2 launch wizard to CreateKeyPair and CreateSecurityGroup (allowing TCP/22 from 0.0.0.0/0) and launched EC2 instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker began to use the AWS web console to create EC2 instances... CreateKeyPair and CreateSecurityGroup

attack_chain.description: Attacker successfully launched 13 xlarge EC2 instances across 5 regions with CloudWatch detailed monitoring disabled, to perform cryptomining.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker successfully created thirteen (13) ec2 instances in five (5) different regions

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker successfully created thirteen (13) ec2 instances in five (5) different regions
the attacker successfully created thirteen (13) ec2 instances in five (5) different regions

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of Gitlab vulnerability CVE-2021-22205 exposed sensitive data containing an AWS admin access key, which the attacker used to access the victim's AWS environment.
persistence_mechanism: Created a new IAM user named 'backup' with a new access key and attached an inline policy granting Action:* Resource:* to maintain access if the original stolen key was revoked.
privilege_escalation: Attached an inline IAM policy (PutUserPolicy 'backupuser') granting full administrative privileges (Action:*, Resource:*) to the newly created 'backup' IAM user.
